meaning: adj.[医] 患炮弹休克的,患战斗疲劳症的
examples: Czech captain Tomas Ujfalusi was left as stunned as his shellshocked team-mates.
"I was shellshocked. I took a bath and just cried," said Boyd. "I knew that I would lose my male husband."
When Frodo returns to the Shire, his quest at an end, he resembles not so much the conquering hero as a shellshocked veteran.
This has left many investors shellshocked, while traders are either quite happy or quite frustrated.
As a shellshocked Hewitt left the court, his fans were in a similar state.
